authoritative_sources.title: About Us
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAOB is an official IATF oversight office that publicly states it oversees and supports consistent application of IATF 16949 to improve quality and performance in the global automotive supply chain.
used_for: why automotive quality and delivery controls are authoritative for this lane
separating coordination from formal quality-system authority
grounding adjacent-lane routing to the IATF ecosystem

authoritative_sources.title: Performance Complaints
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAOB's public complaint workflow is authoritative for when chronic quality, delivery, or process failures under an IATF 16949-certified QMS escalate only after standard internal processes and leadership approval.
used_for: routing boundary between logistics coordination and formal supplier escalation

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 565 - Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 49 CFR Part 565 is the current federal rule for VIN assignment, uniqueness, check digit, physical placement, plant-of-manufacture coding, and transfer-document handling, making it authoritative for vehicle identity evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Vehicle identity and authoritative record validation
used_for: identity artifact expectations before handoff or closure

authoritative_sources.title: VIN Decoder
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NHTSA's VIN decoder is an official agency operating reference for decoding manufacturer-reported VIN data, including build-plant information used in downstream vehicle logistics checks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: VIN lookup and identity confirmation during coordination
used_for: evidence normalization for plant and build context

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 573 - Defect and Noncompliance Responsibility and Reports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 49 CFR Part 573 is the current federal rule for safety-related defect and noncompliance reporting, recall campaign information, and sale or lease prohibitions for affected vehicles and equipment.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Hold, recall, noncompliance, and release-boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Track & Trace
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Odette is a recognized automotive supply-chain standards body. Its public Track & Trace guidance is authoritative for finished-vehicle distribution, shipment tracking, identifier capture, and digital message exchange in the automotive supply chain.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system shipment, finished-vehicle, and event-state normalization

authoritative_sources.title: VIN Labels in the Vehicle Distribution Process
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This Odette and ECG recommendation is an authoritative public operating reference for standard VIN-label location, content, barcode standards, and quality expectations in finished-vehicle distribution.
used_for: handoff prerequisites for scan-based vehicle identification

authoritative_sources.title: The Transport Status Report Message
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Odette's public transport status message guidance is authoritative for how automotive partners exchange movement progress and event updates for materials, components, and finished products across the transport chain.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: In-transit status updates and control-tower evidence exchange

authoritative_sources.title: What is the GS1 Traceability Standard?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for interoperable supply-chain identifiers and traceability. Its traceability standard defines the framework, Critical Tracking Events, and Key Data Elements used to structure interoperable traceability systems.
used_for: evidence completeness checks across movement events

authoritative_sources.title: GS1's role in traceability
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1's public traceability guidance is authoritative for how master, transactional, and event data should be identified, captured, and shared between trading partners for supply-chain transparency.
used_for: master, transaction, and event data categories

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture request scope, prior owner, next owner candidate, VIN population, destination, release status, and tenant authority rules before any movement, ETA, or closure claim is made.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Vehicle identity, yard, and transit evidence normalization
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile VIN identity, plant or build context, compound status, gate events, transport milestones, and label or scan evidence across enterprise and partner systems.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team coordination and routing
workflow_stages.description: Build handoff packages, owner transitions, ETA updates, carrier or vendor-risk summaries, and blocker communication without absorbing dispatch or release authority.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Hold, supplier, recall, and release-boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop coordination when quality holds, campaign or recall flags, supplier nonconformance, unapproved substitutions, or conflicting policies change whether vehicles may move.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Mark transfer complete only when the destination owner, proof-of-movement artifacts, and blocker resolution are explicit; otherwise return safely for escalation.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: production orders, build schedules, VIN lists, and destination commitments
yard and compound inventory states, gate-in or gate-out records, dwell reports, and location scans
carrier assignments, load plans, ETA updates, transport status messages, proof-of-pickup records, and proof-of-delivery records
vehicle status codes, VIN labels, barcode or scan evidence, and control-tower tracking logs
quality holds, campaign or recall holds, damage reports, release records, and nonconformance reports
approved-carrier lists, supplier records, shortage signals, and vendor-risk escalation notes
handoff summaries, routing confirmations, blocker logs, and destination-owner acknowledgements
systems_of_record: finished-vehicle logistics or logistics control-tower platform

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for vehicle logistics coordination
organize authoritative VIN, yard, transport, supplier, and hold records into handoff packages and blocker summaries
summarize logistics availability, ETA, carrier constraints, and release dependencies without implying dispatch or approval authority
escalate: quality hold, damage disposition, release, campaign, recall, or stop-sale decisions requiring formal quality or legal authority
supplier approval, carrier substitution approval, engineering change approval, or transport-compliance authority
conflicting public and tenant policy that materially changes release, hold, export, customs, or ownership handling
refuse: bypass traceability, release, recall, campaign, hold, safety, or quality controls to preserve ETA or throughput
approve movement of held or unreleased vehicles
treat supplier nonconformance, transport substitution, or stop-sale removal as coordination-only when formal approval is required

domain_failure_modes: A vehicle handoff proceeds without authoritative VIN, release, destination, or destination-owner evidence.
Conflicting ERP, YMS, TMS, and destination records are collapsed into a single confident answer instead of a blocker.
A quality hold, campaign hold, stop-sale, or recall boundary is treated as a scheduling inconvenience rather than a formal stop condition.
Supplier nonconformance, unapproved carrier substitution, or alternate routing approval is handled as routine coordination instead of routed to the correct authority.
The lane absorbs dispatch, transport execution, plant release, or system-update ownership instead of preserving coordination boundaries.
Closure is declared without proof that the right destination owner accepted the VIN population and required artifact set.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Tenant-specific adaptation while preserving the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Carrier, rail, port, customs, and country-specific transport restrictions vary materially across trade lanes and cannot be inferred from the public baseline alone.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve tenant route books, customs rules, and approved-carrier constraints before asserting movement-specific routing commitments.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Damaged-EV, battery, hazardous transport, and embargo handling often depend on carrier, insurer, and jurisdiction-specific rules outside the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack requires escalation when these facts change whether a vehicle may move and does not guess transport-compliance posture.

pack_usage_notes: The source mix covers automotive quality governance, vehicle identity, finished-vehicle workflow terminology, cross-party traceability, and hold or release boundaries instead of relying on generic logistics guidance alone.
No unresolved ambiguity is blocking for validated release because the remaining open items are explicitly delegated to tenant retrieval and orchestrator return.
